result.markdown: --- title: "Hydraulics inner-loop benchmark" description: "Timing the new plant hydraulics path against the FF16 baseline across a leaf-area-index sweep — an expensive run, pinned to its own environment." date: 2026-06-10 plant-ref: "develop" plant-sha: "a1b2c3d4e5f6a7b8" categories: [hydraulics, performance, develop] reading-time: true execute: cache: true --- `plant @develop a1b2c3d`{=html} This post is built against an **unreleased** commit on `develop`, so the badge pins to a SHA rather than a version. The simulation below is slow, so it's pinned to its own `renv` snapshot as well — a later project-wide package upgrade can't change this result even on a forced re-run. ## Per-post environment This directory carries its own lockfile, captured once when the post was written: ```r # run from this post's directory, once: renv::snapshot(lockfile = "renv.lock.dev", prompt = FALSE) ``` `plant_session_info()` (footer) reports `renv.lock.dev` when present, so the page records exactly which environment produced the figures. To reproduce: check out `plant` at the SHA above, `renv::restore(lockfile = "renv.lock.dev")`, delete this post's `_freeze/` entry, re-render. ## The sweep ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(plant) library(bench) lai_grid <- seq(1, 8, by = 0.5) results <- lapply(lai_grid, function(L) { bench::mark( baseline = run_inner_ff16(lai = L), hydraulic = run_inner_hydraulics(lai = L), iterations = 20, check = FALSE ) }) ``` ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # ... ggplot of median time by method across lai_grid ... ``` ::: ::: {.callout-note} Numbers here are frozen to commit `a1b2c3d`. When this work lands on `master`, supersede this post with a release-pinned version rather than editing it — keeping the develop snapshot as the historical record. ::: ## Reproducibility footer ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} plant_session_info() ``` :::
